LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER PIETRO SGROMO'S HEARING TO COMPEL ARBITRATION CAL., CIV. PROC. CODE 1281.2. PETITIONER PIETRO PASQUALE ANTONIO SGROMO'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THE EXISTENCE OF A VALID AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE COURT FINDS ON THE BASIS OF THE RECORD, INCLUDING PETITIONER'S OWN DECLARATION, THAT NO SUCH AGREEMENT WAS EVER ENTERED INTO. PROPOSED DRAFT AGREEMENTS THAT ARE EXCHANGED BUT NOT FINALIZED OR SIGNED CANNOT GIVE RISE TO AN ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. (BANNER ENTERTAINMENT, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 62 CAL.APP.4TH 348, 359-362.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER PIETRO PASQUALE ANTONIO SGROMO'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S JANUARY 29, 2019 ORDER DENYING HIS AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO SHOW ANY NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW THAN THOSE BEFORE THE COURT AT THE TIME OF THE ORIGINAL RULING. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1008(B).) PETITIONER'S MOTION IS PREMISED ON AN UNSIGNED MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING THAT WAS BEFORE THE COURT ON THE FIRST MOTION (SGROMO DECL., EXH. 15), AND IT DOES NOT SUPPORT HIS POSITION IN ANY EVENT. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. calendar.matters:
Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Reconsider Order Denying Moving Party'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Pietro PA Sgromo (604-287-5676) petitioner in propria persona via court call.
Craig C. Crockett, esq. (415-477-5724) of Troutman Sanders for respondents .

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER PIETRO SGROMO's Motion For Reconsider Order Denying Moving Party'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Cal Civ. Proc Code 1008.

Petitioner Pietro Pasquale Antonio Sgromo's motion for reconsideration of the Court's January 29, 2019 Order denying his amended petition to compel arbitration is denied. Petitioner has failed to show any new or different facts, circumstances or law than those before the Court at the time of the original ruling. (Code Civ. Proc. 1008(b).) Petitioner's motion is premised on an unsigned Memorandum of Understanding that was before the Court on the first motion (Sgromo Decl., Exh. 15), and it does not support his position in any event. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing To Compel Arbitration Cal., Civ. Proc. Code 1281.2
Off Calendar Jan-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pieetro PA Sgromo, Telephone 415-797-2376, appearing as plaintiff on his own behalf;(CourtCall)
Craig C. Crockett, Esq., Troutman Sanders, Telephoen 415-4775724, appearing for Defendant Polygroup International;

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER PIETRO SGROMO's Hearing To Compel Arbitration Cal., Civ. Proc. Code 1281.2.

Petitioner Pietro Pasquale Antonio Sgromo's petition to compel arbitration is denied. Petitioner has failed to carry its burden of establishing the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate between the parties. The Court finds on the basis of the record, including Petitioner's own declaration, that no such agreement was ever entered into. Proposed draft agreements that are exchanged but not finalized or signed cannot give rise to an enforceable arbitration agreement. (Banner Entertainment, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 348, 359-362.) Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com =(302/HEK)
